cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this problem. So, we have a table with penguins and their details: name, age, height, and weight. The question is asking how many penguins are less than 8 years old and weigh less than 12 kg. The options are from 1 to 5. Let me start by listing out each penguin and check their age and weight. First, let me recall the data. The penguins are Louis, Bernard, Vincent, and Gwen. Let me write down their details again to make sure I don't mix them up. Louis: age 7, weight 11 kg. Bernard: age 5, weight 13 kg. Vincent: age 9, weight 11 kg. Gwen: age 8, weight 15 kg. Now, the criteria are two-fold: age less than 8 and weight less than 12 kg. So, both conditions must be satisfied for a penguin to be counted. Let's check each one. Starting with Louis. His age is 7, which is less than 8. His weight is 11 kg, which is less than 12. So Louis meets both conditions. That's one penguin. Next, Bernard. Age is 5, which is definitely less than 8. His weight is 13 kg. Wait, 13 is more than 12, so he doesn't meet the weight condition. So Bernard doesn't count. Then Vincent. Age is 9. Oh, 9 is more than 8, so he doesn't meet the age condition. Even though his weight is 11 kg, which is under 12, the age disqualifies him. So Vincent isn't counted. Gwen is 8 years old. The condition is less than 8, so 8 is not less than 8. So her age doesn't meet the requirement. Her weight is 15, which is way over 12. So Gwen doesn't count either. So, only Louis meets both conditions. Let me double-check. Louis is 7 (under 8) and 11 kg (under 12). Bernard is under 8 in age but over 12 in weight. Vincent is over
